_comment: Source and dependency currency registry. Web-content sources are checked via source_currency.py (report/check[/--detect-changes] -> web-intel -> mark-checked). P33 added two dependency categories: software-dependency (pip packages, system binaries) and mcp-server (third-party MCP servers), checked token-free by tools/dependency_currency.py against PyPI/GitHub via the optional fields upstream_api (pypi|github_releases|binary|manual), check_url, package, pinned_constraint, validated_version, latest_seen, latest_seen_date. Written only through tools/registry_io.py (source_currency.py + traversal_engine.py accept). Do not hand-edit.

sources.name: Google Search Central Blog
sources.extraction_hint: Look for algorithm update announcements, ranking signal changes, structured data spec updates, and Core Web Vitals threshold changes.

sources.name: YouTube Creator Blog
sources.extraction_hint: Look for algorithm changes affecting discovery, Shorts monetization updates, chapter and timestamps spec changes, and creator program policy updates.

sources.name: Google Search Status Dashboard
sources.extraction_hint: Check for active or recent incidents that could explain ranking flux. Flag to seo-keywords when an incident is active.

sources.name: Search Engine Journal
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for algorithm update coverage, YouTube SEO guides, and home decor content strategy articles. Flag T3 confidence on any data cited from here.

sources.name: Search Engine Roundtable
sources.extraction_hint: Early signals on Google algorithm changes (often surfaces before official confirmation). Useful for trend-check when SEJ has nothing.

sources.name: YouTube Help: Video formats and specs
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to: accepted file formats, resolution limits, aspect ratio specs, upload size limits, Shorts length limits.

sources.name: YouTube Help: Titles, descriptions, and tags
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to title char limits, description char limits, tag count and length limits, chapter timestamp rules.

sources.name: Instagram Help: Reels formats and captions
sources.extraction_hint: Check for caption character limit changes, Reel length limit changes, cover image specs, hashtag strategy updates.

sources.name: TikTok Creator Portal
sources.extraction_hint: Check for video length changes, caption char limit updates, algorithm guidance for home decor niche, monetization threshold changes.

sources.name: Pinterest Business: Pin formats and specs
sources.extraction_hint: Check for title char limit changes, description char limit changes, idea pin format updates, alt text requirements.

sources.name: YouTube Data API v3 Revision History
sources.extraction_hint: Look for deprecated fields or endpoints (especially captions.list, videos.list, search.list), quota changes, and new scopes or parameters.

sources.name: Meta Graph API Changelog
sources.extraction_hint: Check for deprecated fields (e.g. impressions deprecated April 2025, replaced by views), new Instagram Login scope changes, Graph API version deprecations.

sources.name: TikTok for Developers API Changelog
sources.extraction_hint: Check for Display API endpoint changes, new fields in /v2/video/list/, access scope changes, quota limit updates.

sources.name: Microsoft Graph API Changelog
sources.extraction_hint: Check for /me/drive/ endpoint changes, OneDrive file access permission scope updates, deprecated fields or versions.

sources.name: Anthropic Release Notes
sources.extraction_hint: Check for new model releases, MCP spec updates, tool use changes, context window changes, and deprecated API versions.

sources.name: Influencer Marketing Hub Benchmark Report
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: CPM rates by subscriber tier, rate ranges for sponsored YouTube videos and integrations, home decor niche premium factors. Note the report year.

sources.name: Later Creator Economy Report
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: Instagram Reel sponsored rate ranges by follower tier, engagement rate benchmarks by niche, brand deal structure trends.

sources.name: Linqia State of Influencer Marketing
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: content bundle deal structure trends, Pinterest rate benchmarks, negotiation norms, usage rights pricing.

sources.name: CreatorIQ Annual Benchmark Report
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: engagement rate benchmarks by platform and niche, performance benchmarks for home decor category, brand spend trends.

sources.name: Claude Code Release Notes
sources.extraction_hint: Check for new slash commands, MCP server support changes, permission model updates, hook system changes.

sources.name: MCP Specification (modelcontextprotocol.io)
sources.extraction_hint: Check for protocol version changes, new transport mechanisms, resource/tool/prompt type updates that affect skill plugin compatibility.

sources.name: Google Drive MCP Connector Docs
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to available tool names, authentication flow changes, file access permission scope updates.

sources.name: YouTube Help: How recommendations work
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: current recommendation signals, what YouTube says it rewards, what it says creators should focus on. This is the official source for algorithm signal claims.

sources.name: YouTube Help: Understand and improve channel analytics
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: metric definitions (CTR, AVD, impressions, watch time), how to read the traffic sources report, click-through benchmarks, audience retention curve interpretation.

sources.name: YouTube Creator Academy: Grow your channel
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for new lessons on thumbnail strategy, title optimization, end screen and card best practices, Shorts growth guidance. Traversal depth 2 to capture linked studies.

sources.name: Google Search Central: SEO Starter Guide
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: title tag best practices, meta description guidance, structured data recommendations, mobile-first indexing requirements. Traversal finds linked technical documentation.

sources.name: Google Search Central: Video best practices
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: VideoObject schema requirements, video sitemap specs, thumbnail requirements for video rich results, key moments / chapter markup.

sources.name: Google Structured Data: VideoObject schema
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: required and recommended VideoObject properties, Clip and SeekToAction markup for chapter links, testing tool URL. Changes here affect how videos appear in Google Search.

sources.name: Google Search Central: How Google evaluates content quality
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: E-E-A-T signals (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness), helpful content criteria, what qualifies as low-quality or thin content. Crucial for content strategy quality gates.

sources.name: Pinterest Creator Hub: SEO and discovery
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: current Pinterest search trends and predictions, keyword guidance for home decor category, algorithm changes to pin distribution. Annual Predicts report is high priority.

sources.name: Pinterest Newsroom
sources.extraction_hint: Check for algorithm changes, new ad format announcements, creator program updates. Early signal for platform spec changes before help center updates.

sources.name: TikTok Creator Portal: Understanding the For You Page
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: what TikTok says drives FYP distribution (completion rate, interactions, video info, device/account settings), content quality guidance, hashtag role.

sources.name: TikTok Newsroom
sources.extraction_hint: Check for algorithm transparency updates, new creator monetization features, policy changes. Early signal before creator portal updates.

sources.name: FTC: Disclosures 101 for Social Media Influencers
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: required disclosure language, placement requirements (beginning of description, verbal in video), what counts as a material connection. Changes here affect safety.md and deal contract templates.

sources.name: FTC: Endorsement Guides
sources.extraction_hint: Check for updates to the formal endorsement guides (16 CFR Part 255). Pay attention to social media platform-specific guidance and AI-generated content rules.

sources.name: Moz Blog: YouTube SEO and video search
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for video SEO tactical guides, CTR optimization studies, keyword research methodology articles. Flag T3 confidence. Traversal finds cited studies and data sources.

sources.name: Ahrefs Blog: Video SEO
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: video ranking factor studies, keyword research for YouTube, competitor video analysis methodology. Flag T3. Traversal finds linked research.

sources.name: vidIQ Creator Academy
sources.extraction_hint: YouTube-specific SEO tactics, keyword research methodology, Shorts optimization. Lower confidence than T1/T2 but useful for niche-specific YouTube tactics. Traversal finds trending keyword examples.

sources.name: The Spruce: Home Decor
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for trending home decor topics, new style names (e.g. '[trending style name]', '[trending style name]'), DIY technique coverage. Extract: what is being written about, what angles are saturated, what is underserved. High editorial authority in the niche.

sources.name: Apartment Therapy
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for renter-friendly DIY coverage, small-space organization, vintage and thrifting trend articles. Strong overlap with The Renter and Vintage Hunter personas. High editorial authority.

sources.name: HGTV: Decorating ideas
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: which room types and project types receive the most editorial coverage, which aesthetic terms appear in headlines, seasonal and trend features. Use for competitor-scan context.

sources.name: Better Homes and Gardens: Decorating
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: trending style names, DIY and makeover approaches being covered, seasonal content patterns. BHG is a mainstream authority whose coverage signals broad market awareness of a trend.

sources.name: Sprout Social: Social media statistics and benchmarks
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: engagement rate benchmarks by platform and industry (home/lifestyle niche), best posting times, reach benchmarks. Note report date and sample size if stated.

sources.name: Buffer: State of Social Media Report
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: platform-level engagement benchmarks, creator posting frequency data, video vs. image performance comparisons. Annual report — note year.

sources.name: TikTok Content Posting API
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to upload endpoint URLs, rate limit values, new required fields (especially is_aigc), scope name changes, and chunked upload protocol updates.

sources.name: TikTok Research API
sources.extraction_hint: Check for new fields in video/comment queries (favorites_count added May 2026, display_name added May 2026), quota changes, and access tier requirement changes.

sources.name: TikTok Display API
sources.extraction_hint: Check for endpoint URL changes, rate limit updates, and new fields in /v2/video/list/ or /v2/user/info/.

sources.name: TikTok Login Kit
sources.extraction_hint: Check for OAuth flow changes, token lifetime changes (access token 24h, refresh 365d as of 2026), and new platform support.

sources.name: TikTok Newsroom: How TikTok recommends videos
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: official FYP ranking signals (rewatch rate now #1, surpassing completion), what TikTok says are non-factors (follower count, prior video performance), signals that make content FYP-ineligible. This is the primary official source for algorithm transparency.

sources.name: Meta Graph API: Instagram content publishing
sources.extraction_hint: Check for: new media_type values, new post_info fields (trial_params added December 2025), metric deprecations (impressions deprecated April 2025, replaced by views), scope name changes (instagram_business_basic is current). Note API version.

sources.name: Instagram: How Reels are ranked and recommended
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: official ranking signals for Reels (sends per reach as unique primary signal, watch time, saves, originality gate at 10+ reposts), what Instagram says it penalizes (low-res, watermarked content, reposts), hashtag guidance updates.

sources.name: Pinterest API v5 Documentation
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to: video pin upload flow (register → S3 upload → create pin), rate limit values (reads 1000/min, writes 100/min, analytics 200/min), scope name changes, and new analytics metrics.

sources.name: YouTube Help: YouTube Shorts
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to: maximum Shorts length (3 minutes as of Oct 2024), YPP Shorts eligibility thresholds (1,000 subs + 10M views in 90 days), revenue share percentages, and any changes to how Shorts are identified.

sources.name: YouTube Creator Blog: How Shorts are distributed
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: current primary Shorts ranking signal (session contribution is now primary as of 2025, replacing swipe-through-rate), AVD thresholds for distribution, three-phase distribution model, freshness decay window (~28-30 days). Signal stack changes over time.

sources.name: Google Trends
sources.extraction_hint: Use gprop='youtube' parameter for YouTube-specific trend data. Check home decor and DIY seasonal patterns. Breakout keywords (>5000% growth) signal emerging demand before it appears in autocomplete. All volume data labeled [estimated, unverified].

sources.name: Sprout Social: Social media industry benchmarks
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: engagement rate benchmarks by platform and industry vertical (home/lifestyle), optimal posting frequency data, reach benchmarks for micro-creators. Note report date and methodology.

sources.name: vidIQ: YouTube Shorts optimization guide
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for CTR benchmark data (vidIQ analyzed 5.08M channels: <3% poor, 3-5% good, 7%+ excellent), Shorts algorithm updates, hook format recommendations with retention data. Flag T3 confidence.

sources.name: TubeBuddy: YouTube growth tips
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for: SEO optimization tactics, chapter marker best practices, end screen strategy, keyword research approaches for YouTube. Flag T3 confidence.

sources.name: Pinterest API v5 create-pin
sources.extraction_hint: pins-create endpoint: media registration, board_id, required scopes (pins:write); watch for v6 or field changes

sources.name: YouTube Data API v3 videos.insert
sources.extraction_hint: resumable upload endpoint, quota cost, required scopes; watch for quota changes

sources.name: Instagram Content Publishing API
sources.extraction_hint: two-step container then media_publish flow; rate limits; trial_params; watch for Graph API version sunsets

sources.name: FTC Endorsement Guides: What People Are Asking (FAQ)
sources.extraction_hint: practical dos and don'ts on disclosure placement, wording, and platform tools; answers common creator questions

sources.name: FTC Endorsements, Influencers, and Reviews hub
sources.extraction_hint: landing page linking current FTC endorsement guidance and enforcement updates for influencers

sources.name: U.S. Copyright Office: What Is Copyright
sources.extraction_hint: copyright ownership basics; who holds rights in created content; useful for usage-rights and ownership clause review

sources.name: Cornell LII Wex: Contract
sources.extraction_hint: plain-language definition of contract terms and formation; general reference only, not case law

sources.name: Cornell LII Wex: License
sources.extraction_hint: plain-language definition of a license and its scope; supports usage-rights and licensing clause review

sources.name: FTC Soliciting and Paying for Online Reviews: A Guide for Marketers
sources.extraction_hint: rules on paid and incentivized reviews and endorsements; relevant to gifted and affiliate deal terms

sources.name: The Home Depot product pricing (DIY materials and tools)
sources.extraction_hint: current retail prices for project materials (lumber, paint, hardware, fasteners) and tools; per-item vendor pricing pages, not aggregators

sources.name: Lowe's product pricing (DIY materials and tools)
sources.extraction_hint: current retail prices for project materials and tools; per-item vendor pricing pages for cross-vendor comparison against Home Depot

sources.name: MCP Python SDK (mcp)
sources.extraction_hint: SDK release notes for transport/auth and tool-registration API changes tracked to the MCP spec
sources._why: the local MCP server (tools/mcp_server.py) is built on this SDK; a transport or tool-schema change in lockstep with the MCP spec breaks tool exposure

sources.name: PySceneDetect (scenedetect)
sources.extraction_hint: ContentDetector / scene_manager API changes; the get_seconds()->seconds rename in 0.7
sources._why: pinned >=0.7,<0.8; a 0.8/major bump renames API (get_seconds->seconds shim precedent) and breaks scene detection

sources.name: MoviePy (moviepy)
sources.extraction_hint: breaking renames; removal of moviepy.editor; a v3 major
sources._why: pinned >=2,<3; v2 was a breaking rewrite (renamed methods, removed moviepy.editor). A v3 would break the shorts render lane

sources.name: OpenTimelineIO (opentimelineio)
sources.extraction_hint: schema version bumps; adapter packaging (adapters split into separate pip packages since 0.17/0.18)
sources._why: the neutral timeline core round-trips through OTIO; a schema or adapter-packaging change breaks build/parse

sources.name: OTIO FCPX XML adapter (otio-fcpx-xml-adapter)
sources.extraction_hint: adapter API/compat with the installed opentimelineio core
sources._why: FCPXML round-trip depends on this adapter matching the OTIO core version

sources.name: OTIO Kdenlive adapter (otio-kdenlive-adapter)
sources.extraction_hint: early 0.0.x adapter; API is unstable, watch every release
sources._why: the MLT/Kdenlive read path uses this pre-1.0 adapter; any release can change the API

sources.name: PyAV (av)
sources.extraction_hint: libav/ffmpeg ABI compatibility; a major that drops an ffmpeg series
sources._why: windowed-RMS silence backend; PyAV tracks the ffmpeg ABI, so an ffmpeg bump can force a PyAV bump

sources.name: OpenCV headless (opencv-python-headless)
sources.extraction_hint: the backend PySceneDetect uses; a major that changes cv2 imports
sources._why: PySceneDetect ContentDetector runs on OpenCV; an incompatible major breaks scene detection

sources.name: Playwright (playwright)
sources.extraction_hint: bundled Chromium version and the sync API surface used by acquire.py
sources._why: JavaScript-render path for competitor snapshots; the Chromium bundle and API drift

sources.name: Requests (requests)
sources.extraction_hint: stable HTTP client; only a major (3.x) matters
sources._why: HTTP client under the fetch stack; low churn, long interval

sources.name: charset-normalizer
sources.extraction_hint: encoding detection used by the fetch stack; only a major matters
sources._why: charset detection for fetched pages; low churn

sources.name: BeautifulSoup (beautifulsoup4)
sources.extraction_hint: HTML parse API used by the competitor metadata parser; only a major matters
sources._why: parses competitor page HTML (SIGI_STATE, og tags); stable API, long interval

sources.name: FFmpeg / ffprobe (system binary)
sources.extraction_hint: silencedetect / scdet filter output format and flag names; a major that changes stderr shape
sources._why: core media backend; the committed silencedetect/scdet stderr fixtures are parsed by regex, so a filter/flag change breaks detection. Not pip-installed; check the release page manually

sources.name: MLT framework / melt (system binary)
sources.extraction_hint: MLT XML schema and melt CLI changes; new avformat/ffmpeg support
sources._why: Shotcut/Kdenlive render lane; the MLT XML we emit and the melt CLI we shell out to can drift

sources.name: DaVinci Resolve Scripting API
sources.extraction_hint: the in-install Developer/Scripting/README.txt; the supported Python interpreter range
sources._why: Lane B (live Resolve control) is Studio-only and pins Python 3.10 to 3.12; a Resolve major that moves the interpreter breaks the fusionscript bridge

sources.name: Apple FCPXML reference / DTD
sources.extraction_hint: FCPXML version bumps (FCP 11 -> 1.13, 12.x -> ~1.14); DTD element changes
sources.pinned_constraint: version read from the installed FCP DTD
sources._why: fcpxml.py reads the target version from the on-disk DTD; an FCP major bumps the FCPXML version and can change validation

sources.name: Apple Compressor CLI
sources.extraction_hint: the Compressor -batchname/-jobpath CLI flags
sources._why: macOS batch-export lane; the CLI flag surface can change across Compressor versions

sources.name: CommandPost (macOS automation)
sources.extraction_hint: the Lua automation surface CommandPost exposes
sources._why: the escape hatch for FCP UI-driven steps; its automation API can change

sources.name: Google Workspace MCP (workspace-mcp)
sources.extraction_hint: tool names, OAuth 2.1 scopes, and tool-tier flags used by the Google connectors and the wizard
sources._why: the actual server backing every Google Workspace connector; a tool-name or auth change breaks Gmail/Calendar/Drive setup

sources.name: Microsoft 365 MCP (Softeria ms-365-mcp-server)
sources.extraction_hint: device-code auth flow and tool names for Outlook/Calendar/Excel/OneDrive
sources._why: the server backing every Microsoft 365 connector; auth-flow or tool-name drift breaks Outlook/Excel/OneDrive setup

sources.name: Wolfram Alpha MCP (mcp-wolfram-alpha)
sources.extraction_hint: tool names and the App ID auth; release page slug pending confirmation
sources._why: exact symbolic computation lane; a tool-name change silently drops it to guidance-only

sources.name: E2B Code Interpreter MCP
sources.extraction_hint: sandbox API and tool names; release page slug pending confirmation
sources._why: sandboxed Python for statistical analysis; an API change drops the compute lane to guidance-only

sources.name: stats-compass-mcp
sources.extraction_hint: the 50+ pandas tool names for t-tests/ANOVA/chi-square
sources._why: the pandas hypothesis-testing lane; a tool-name change drops it to guidance-only

sources.name: DuckDB / MotherDuck MCP (mcp-server-motherduck)
sources.extraction_hint: SQL tool names and local vs MotherDuck mode
sources._why: analytical SQL over CSV/Parquet/JSON; a tool-name change drops the data-query lane

sources.name: Jupyter MCP (jupyter-mcp-server)
sources.extraction_hint: persistent-notebook tool names; release API pending confirmation
sources._why: persistent notebook sessions for multi-step computation; API drift drops the lane

sources.name: R Statistics MCP (rmcp)
sources.extraction_hint: the 52 R statistical tool names; release API/slug pending confirmation
sources._why: advanced R modeling lane; a tool-name change drops it to guidance-only

sources.name: Monte Carlo Simulation MCP (MCS-MCP)
sources.extraction_hint: simulation tool names; release API/slug pending confirmation
sources._why: probabilistic modeling and revenue forecasting; an API change drops the lane

sources.name: scikit-learn MCP (mcp-server-scikit-learn)
sources.extraction_hint: ML classification/regression/clustering tool names; release API/slug pending confirmation
sources._why: ML prediction lane for audience segmentation and trend prediction; API drift drops it to guidance-only

sources.name: FTC Endorsement Guides, eCFR 16 CFR Part 255 (live text)
sources.extraction_hint: live authoritative 16 CFR Part 255 text; the 2024 amendments and any staff guidance on AI / virtual-influencer endorsements; clear-and-conspicuous disclosure

sources.name: Amazon (materials pricing)
sources.extraction_hint: list price for a named product/model at fetch time; cite the URL and date, never a remembered price

sources.name: Rust-Oleum paint and finishes (pricing reference)
sources.extraction_hint: paint/finish product lines and MSRP where listed; retailer price is the authority for actual cost

sources.name: Michaels (craft materials pricing)
sources.extraction_hint: craft and decor materials list price at fetch time; cite URL and date

sources.name: GitHub OSS SEO methodology (curated crawl)
sources.extraction_hint: notable open-source SEO/keyword tooling and methodology repos (SERP-overlap clustering, PAA expansion, topical maps); re-crawl for new techniques feeding github-seo-methodology.json

sources.name: Florida Building Code (official portal)
sources.extraction_hint: adopted edition banner (2023 FBC 8th Edition, 2021 I-Codes + ASCE 7-22); watch for the 9th Edition (2026) transition; single statewide code with local amendments

sources.name: Florida Building Code amendments and declaratory statements search
sources.extraction_hint: statewide amendments, errata, and declaratory statements between code cycles

sources.name: Florida Building Code Residential 2023 (ICC Digital Codes, free viewer)
sources.extraction_hint: free read-only viewer for FBC-R section numbers; cite sections, never copy text

sources.name: Florida Product Approval system
sources.extraction_hint: statewide product approval; HVHZ requires a Miami-Dade NOA; wind-borne debris opening protection

sources.name: Miami-Dade Product Control (NOA) search
sources.extraction_hint: High-Velocity Hurricane Zone Notice of Acceptance; TAS 201/202/203 impact protocols for Miami-Dade and Broward

sources.name: Florida Statutes Chapter 553 (Building Construction Standards)
sources.extraction_hint: statutory basis for the FBC; public domain state statute (may be cited and quoted)

sources.name: NC OSFM building codes (current and past)
sources.extraction_hint: 2018 NC Residential Code (2015 IRC) is enforced now; 2024 NC RC (2021 IRC) adopted but effective date postponed (earliest ~2027)

sources.name: NC Building Code Council / Residential Code Council
sources.extraction_hint: watch the 2024 code effective-date decisions and the new Residential Code Council certification timeline

sources.name: NC OSFM news and press releases
sources.extraction_hint: official announcements of code edition transitions and effective dates

sources.name: North Carolina Residential Code 2018 (ICC Digital Codes, free viewer)
sources.extraction_hint: free read-only viewer for NC RC section numbers (2015 IRC base); includes Chapter 45 High Wind Zones

sources.name: NC Office of Administrative Hearings (rulemaking / NCAC)
sources.extraction_hint: administrative rule adoptions affecting code effective dates; public-domain state records

sources.name: International Residential Code 2021 (ICC Digital Codes, free viewer)
sources.extraction_hint: model residential code both FL (2021 base) and pending NC 2024 derive from; cite sections R-numbers, never copy text or tables

sources.name: International Energy Conservation Code 2021 (ICC Digital Codes)
sources.extraction_hint: insulation R-value and fenestration tables by climate zone (N1102 / R402); cite sections, use the public-domain DOE BASC restatement for values

sources.name: International Plumbing/Mechanical/Fuel Gas Codes (ICC Digital Codes)
sources.extraction_hint: IPC (drain slope, DFU), IMC and IFGC references for plumbing/HVAC/gas; cite sections only

sources.name: ICC Digital Codes library (all I-Codes landing)
sources.extraction_hint: catches new I-Code editions and newly adopted state codes as they publish

sources.name: NFPA 70 National Electrical Code (free access)
sources.extraction_hint: NEC section numbers (210.52, 210.8, 210.12, 314.16, 240.4); free registered read-only; never copy text

sources.name: UpCodes Florida (aggregated code viewer)
sources.extraction_hint: secondary free viewer for FL adopted codes and amendments; cross-check against the official portal

sources.name: UpCodes North Carolina (aggregated code viewer)
sources.extraction_hint: secondary free viewer for NC adopted codes; confirm the enforced edition against OSFM

sources.name: AWC DCA6 Prescriptive Residential Wood Deck Construction Guide
sources.extraction_hint: deck ledger attachment, joist/beam spans, footing, lateral load; AWC-copyrighted, cite by figure/table, never copy the tables

sources.name: AWC Wood Frame Construction Manual (WFCM)
sources.extraction_hint: prescriptive framing, fastening, and wind/seismic connections; AWC-copyrighted, cite by section

sources.name: AWC Span Calculator and design tools
sources.extraction_hint: official joist/rafter span tool; link the tool, do not reproduce its tables

sources.name: APA - The Engineered Wood Association resource library
sources.extraction_hint: panel span ratings, sheathing, subflooring; APA terms forbid AI training and redistribution, cite by publication only

sources.name: ACCA Manual J / S / D (residential HVAC design standards)
sources.extraction_hint: Manual J load, Manual S equipment selection, Manual D duct design; ACCA-copyrighted, cite the standard, never size by rule of thumb

sources.name: Building Science Corporation document library
sources.extraction_hint: moisture, air sealing, wall assemblies, climate-specific detailing; cite articles, respect their copyright

sources.name: Gypsum Association GA-216 (application and finishing of gypsum panels)
sources.extraction_hint: drywall fastener spacing, fire-rated assemblies; cite GA-216 by section

sources.name: DOE Building America Solution Center
sources.extraction_hint: public-domain step-by-step trade guides and diagrams (PNNL/DOE); per-image credit lines mark third-party images; A-Z index at ?items_per_page=All

sources.name: DOE BASC minimum insulation requirements by IECC climate zone
sources.extraction_hint: public-domain R-value-by-climate-zone restatement of IECC/IRC Table R402.1.2; authoritative source for insulation.json and build_calc rvalue_zone

sources.name: PNNL guide to determining climate zone by county (IECC 2021)
sources.extraction_hint: public-domain county-to-IECC-climate-zone table for FL and NC; PDF at osti.gov/servlets/purl/1893981; feeds fl-nc-specifics.json

sources.name: OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Construction standards (eCFR)
sources.extraction_hint: public-domain construction safety (fall protection, excavation, scaffolds, PPE); machine-readable XML/API

sources.name: FEMA residential coastal and flood construction guides
sources.extraction_hint: public-domain P-804, FEMA-232; flood elevation, coastal foundations, wind retrofit; link FEMA MSC for per-site BFE

sources.name: CPSC public safety handbooks (guards, stairs, handrails, playgrounds)
sources.extraction_hint: public-domain guard/stair/handrail dimensional guidance corroborating IRC R311/R312

sources.name: USDA Forest Products Laboratory Wood Handbook + PS 20
sources.extraction_hint: public-domain wood mechanical properties and PS 20 nominal-vs-actual lumber sizing basis

sources.name: HUD USER residential construction and rehab publications
sources.extraction_hint: public-domain residential construction, durability, and rehab guides

sources.name: Florida FDACS termite and building code protection
sources.extraction_hint: Florida subterranean termite soil treatment and Certificate of Compliance context for FRC R318

sources.name: NREL openstudio-standards material and construction data
sources.extraction_hint: BSD-3 (attribution): material-layer R-values and wall/roof/floor assembly definitions + IECC-aligned climate zone sets; raw JSON under lib/openstudio-standards/standards/**/data; feeds assemblies.json

sources.name: Wikidata construction terms (CC0 glossary backbone)
sources.extraction_hint: CC0 labels and descriptions for building components/materials/tools (P279* of Q1516079/Q22811462/Q39546); entity API wbsearchentities; feeds glossary.json; no attribution required

sources.name: Wikimedia Commons construction diagram categories
sources.extraction_hint: per-file CC/PD diagrams; record the exact per-file license and attribution in diagram-index.json before bundling

sources.name: Gmail API release notes (Atom feed)
sources.extraction_hint: new dated entries: quota-model changes, deprecations, new endpoints; the feed's newest pubDate is the freshness signal

sources.name: Google Drive API release notes
sources.extraction_hint: Drive v3 additive changes and deprecations; verify the per-page Subscribe feed slug, else ETag+sha256 of the dated entry list

sources.name: Google Sheets API release notes
sources.extraction_hint: Sheets v4 changes; the cross-surface task/freshness store writes append rows here, so field/quota changes matter

sources.name: Pinterest OpenAPI spec releases (GitHub)
sources.extraction_hint: each release documents added endpoints and marks breaking changes; the raw OpenAPI in the repo is the authoritative v5 surface

sources.name: Microsoft Graph metadata (CSDL) commit feed
sources.extraction_hint: schema/property changes in the annotated CSDL catch breaks the prose changelog may lag; pair with the changelog RSS

sources.name: EasyPost client library releases (GitHub)
sources.extraction_hint: mirrors the dated YYYY.MM.DD API versions and carrier decommissions; backs the shipments carrier connector

sources.name: Ship24 tracking API OpenAPI spec
sources.extraction_hint: Ship24 publishes no changelog; sha256 the OpenAPI YAML to detect endpoint/field changes (the only reliable signal)

sources.name: YouTube Analytics API revision history (Atom feed)
sources.extraction_hint: report-version bumps and metric changes (engaged_views, Shorts view redefinition); dated structured entries

sources.name: Model Context Protocol spec releases (GitHub)
sources.extraction_hint: a new date-tag (e.g. 2025-11-25) is a new protocol revision; watch for transport deprecations (SSE->Streamable HTTP), JSON Schema dialect, auth changes

sources.name: MCP Bundle (.mcpb) manifest spec (GitHub)
sources.extraction_hint: the .dxt->.mcpb rename and manifest_version bumps (0.3/0.4); required manifest fields for a Desktop Extension bundle

sources.name: Anthropic Agent Skills repo commits (GitHub)
sources.extraction_hint: no releases; watch commits for skill-format changes; the repo has no tags

sources.name: Agent Skills open specification (SKILL.md contract)
sources.extraction_hint: SKILL.md frontmatter caps (name 64 / description 1024 / compatibility 500 chars), reserved-word ban on 'anthropic'/'claude', no consecutive hyphens; the field-constraint table is what breaks packaging

sources.name: Claude Apps release notes (Projects/Desktop)
sources.extraction_hint: consumer-surface changes to Projects knowledge limits, Memory, Desktop connectors; no RSS, ETag+sha256

sources.name: OpenAI Apps SDK changelog (MCP in ChatGPT)
sources.extraction_hint: the newer ChatGPT app packaging surface (built on MCP); _meta.ui contract, widget MIME, structuredContent

sources.name: OpenAI GPT Actions production limits
sources.extraction_hint: hard caps that silently truncate/reject exports: endpoint/param descriptions 300/700 chars, payloads <100k chars, 45s timeout, no custom headers

sources.name: Google Gemini API changelog (plain text)
sources.extraction_hint: plain-text mirror; cheapest reliable sha256 diff of any surface; tool/agent packaging changes

sources.name: Google Gemini model deprecations
sources.extraction_hint: model retirement dates; any pinned model id in an export fails after its shutdown date

sources.name: Google Search Central blog (RSS)
sources.extraction_hint: core-update announcements and SEO best-practice changes; newest pubDate/guid is the signal; verified valid RSS 2.0

sources.name: Google Search Status Dashboard (ranking/core updates)
sources.extraction_hint: authoritative record of core/ranking-update rollouts with timestamps; exposes both RSS and a JSON history endpoint

sources.name: Google Trends API (alpha)
sources.extraction_hint: first sanctioned programmatic Trends access; JSON, consistent cross-request scaling, ~5yr window to T-2 days; monitor alpha->GA via the Search Central feed

sources.name: Sprout Social social media image sizes guide (datestamped)
sources.extraction_hint: self-describes 'Last Updated: <date>'; the change-alarm for platform specs -> when it moves, re-read the OFFICIAL platform help page to confirm before updating specs.json

sources.name: Hootsuite social media image sizes guide (monthly datestamp)
sources.extraction_hint: title carries [Month Year]; a 2-of-3 cross-check with Sprout to reduce single-source error; confirm against the official page

sources.name: Influencer Marketing Hub benchmark report (annual)
sources.extraction_hint: annual (~May), byline-date on the page; cache derived rate numbers WITH the report publish date + URL, never the page/tables (proprietary)

sources.name: HypeAuditor State of Influencer Marketing (annual)
sources.extraction_hint: first-party platform data; email-gated PDF; a new year-slug returning 200 is the freshness signal; cache facts + attribution only

sources.name: Shopify retail holiday calendar (annual)
sources.extraction_hint: year-stamped retail shopping dates; the DATES are public facts (freely cacheable); re-verify on the year flip in the title

sources.name: FTC Consumer Reviews & Testimonials rule, eCFR 16 CFR Part 465
sources.extraction_hint: the real 2024 legislative rule (effective 2024-10-21): bans fake/AI-generated reviews, undisclosed insider reviews, review suppression; sha256 the Versioner part XML

sources.name: FTC press releases (RSS)
sources.extraction_hint: the primary FTC change signal; filter items for endorsement/reviews/AI/influencer; announces new AI-endorsement actions

sources.name: Federal Register API (FTC endorsement/reviews rulemaking)
sources.extraction_hint: JSON, no key; newest document_number for CFR title 16 FTC actions on Part 255/465

sources.name: US Copyright Office Federal Register notices (RSS)
sources.extraction_hint: USCO rule changes incl. the Copyright-and-AI report series; public domain, free to poll

sources.name: New York synthetic-performer ad disclosure law (S8420)
sources.extraction_hint: first-in-nation ad rule (effective 2026-06-09): conspicuous disclosure when an ad uses a synthetic performer; hash the bill status page or use the NY Senate JSON API

sources.name: California AI Transparency Act (SB 942, amended by AB 853)
sources.extraction_hint: provenance/disclosure duties for large GenAI providers; effective 2026-08-02 (delayed by AB 853); watch for further amending bills

sources.name: DOE Building Energy Codes state certifications (IECC)
sources.extraction_hint: DOE 2024 IECC determination; residential certification deadline 2026-12-30; cache adoption status/dates (facts), never the IECC text

sources.name: ICC I-Code adoption maps (PDF)
sources.extraction_hint: state-by-state adopted I-Code editions; sha256 the PDF bytes; store the adoption FACT only (ICC text is copyrighted, no scraping)

sources.name: FEMA National Flood Hazard Layer - Flood Hazard Zones (ArcGIS REST)
sources.extraction_hint: point flood-zone query on layer 28 (FLD_ZONE, SFHA_TF, STATIC_BFE) in inSR=4326; layer 28 has no editingInfo, so poll the LOMRs layer 1 for change
sources._license: US federal work, public domain (17 USC 105); not the legally authoritative FIRM

sources.name: FEMA NFHL - Letters of Map Revision (change signal)
sources.extraction_hint: LOMR effective/case dates; the regulatory-change signal for NFHL since layer 28 lacks editingInfo

sources.name: South Florida Water Management District open-data GIS (ArcGIS Hub)
sources.extraction_hint: district/basin boundaries + ERP layers; ArcGIS Hub item modified date / REST lastEditDate
sources._license: FL public agency open data; per-item license, generally open/public-domain

sources.name: City of Miami historic districts (ArcGIS open data)
sources.extraction_hint: local historic-district boundary polygons (design-review trigger); Hub item modified / REST lastEditDate
sources._license: municipal public records / open data; per-item license

sources.name: Florida Statute 553.899 - Milestone structural inspections (SB 4D)
sources.extraction_hint: condo/co-op >=3 stories, milestone by year building turns 30 then every 10y; the year in the URL path is the codification-change signal

sources.name: Florida Statute 553.842 - Statewide product approval (HVHZ authority)
sources.extraction_hint: statewide product evaluation/approval; underpins HVHZ product control (Miami-Dade NOA / TAS)

sources.name: Southeast Florida Regional Climate Compact - Unified Sea Level Rise Projection
sources.extraction_hint: the regional SLR projection edition (2019 current, ~5yr revision); the value informs local freeboard ordinances; watch the edition year
sources._license: regional compact report; cite the projection facts + edition, do not mirror the report

sources.name: NC Mountain Ridge Protection Act ridgelines (NC OneMap ArcGIS)
sources.extraction_hint: statewide protected-ridge lines (topo-map digitized, SCREENING ONLY); static unless the MRPA is amended; the FeatureServer editingInfo/item modified is the freshness signal
sources._license: NC OneMap / NCGS open, public; no warranty (accuracy); attribute to NCGS

sources.name: Buncombe County GIS - protected ridges + zoning overlays (ArcGIS)
sources.extraction_hint: layer 7 Protected Ridges, layer 20 County Zoning Overlay, Slope Stability Index Map; steep-slope % itself is DEM-derived (no slope-percent layer)
sources._license: county public records / open GIS; standard accuracy disclaimer; attribute to Buncombe County GIS

sources.name: Rutherford County GIS - reference layers (parcels/flood only)
sources.extraction_hint: parcels + flood zones only; Rutherford has NO countywide zoning and NO steep-slope ordinance, so slope must be DEM-derived
sources._license: county public records / open GIS; standard accuracy disclaimer

sources.name: NC Mountain Ridge Protection Act (Ch. 113A, Article 14)
sources.extraction_hint: protected ridge = elevation >= 3,000 ft AND >= 500 ft above the adjacent valley; tall building > 40 ft; counties/cities opt in

sources.name: Buncombe County zoning ordinance (Ch. 78 Art. VI, steep-slope/ridge overlays)
sources.extraction_hint: Sec. 78-644 Steep Slope/High Elevation (above 2,500 ft, slope <= 35%); Sec. 78-645 Protected Ridge (above 3,000 ft); geotech trigger at existing grade >= 36% or mapped hazard
sources._license: county ordinance = public-domain law; the .gov PDF is fetchable, Municode hosting is not

sources.name: Asheville local historic districts + downtown design review (ArcGIS open data)
sources.extraction_hint: local historic overlay + downtown design review district (Asheville Code Sec. 7-9-2); boundary polygons via the city ArcGIS hub

sources.name: NC water-supply watersheds + river basins (NC OneMap / DEQ)
sources.extraction_hint: NC DEQ water-supply-watershed (WS) protection overlays + major river basins (French Broad, Broad); ArcGIS REST / Hub modified date

sources.name: City of Orlando Local Historic Districts (ArcGIS FeatureServer)
sources.extraction_hint: point-in-polygon on HistoricDistricts; watch editingInfo.lastEditDate for boundary change; re-run tools/geo_source_fetch.py --cache-orlando to refresh cached polygons
sources._license: City of Orlando open data / public records; City disclaims legal-boundary accuracy ('not legally binding','spatially inaccurate'); advisory only

sources.name: City of Orlando Land Use Zoning (ArcGIS FeatureServer)
sources.extraction_hint: point-in-polygon on Zoning + OverlayA-D; watch editingInfo.lastEditDate; setback NUMBERS live in Ch.58 code (not scraped)
sources._license: City of Orlando open data / public records; advisory only

sources.name: Orlando City Code Ch. 62 (Historic Preservation) + COA rule
sources.extraction_hint: cite adopted section (62.200 COA required) + district list; monitor the City page (code text on Municode is ToS-limited, not scraped)
sources._license: public ordinance (Georgia v. Public.Resource.Org); cite the section, do not reproduce Municode text

sources.name: Orlando LDC Traditional City design standards (62.600 to 62.629) + Residential Standards Guide
sources.extraction_hint: cite Traditional City section range + overlay legend; setback values live in Ch.58 tables (Municode, not scraped) -> null-flagged
sources._license: public ordinance; cite section, do not reproduce code text

sources.name: ASCE 7 Hazard Tool / FBC wind maps (Vult, WBDR authoritative source)
sources.extraction_hint: authoritative point Vult (Fig. 1609.3(2)) + WBDR determination; values null-flagged in records until a point query is run
sources._license: ASCE tool; wind-speed FACT is not copyrightable, cite the source

sources.name: St. Johns River WMD open GIS (WMD + major drainage basin boundaries)
sources.extraction_hint: point-in-polygon NAME (SJRWMD) + Major_Drainage_Basin_24K_2013 basin; Orange is a split county (south = SFWMD)
sources._license: Florida public records (SJRWMD open data); advisory only

sources.name: Creator OS ecosystem releases (self-update target)
sources.extraction_hint: Latest published release tag vs the installed VERSION; drives tools/update_check.py
sources._why: The one currency lane that watches the ecosystem itself; a newer release upstream is what update_check reports

sources.name: Claude Code plugins and marketplaces (auto-update mechanics)
sources.extraction_hint: Plugin/marketplace auto-update defaults, extraKnownMarketplaces autoUpdate, /plugin commands, FORCE_AUTOUPDATE_PLUGINS
sources._why: Governs the true-background distribution channel for Claude Code; fast-moving per-version notes

sources.name: Claude Code advanced setup (CLI auto-updater)
sources.extraction_hint: claude update, native background updates, autoUpdatesChannel, DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER, DISABLE_UPDATES
sources._why: The CLI/native auto-update floor for a Claude Code install of Creator OS

sources.name: Claude Cowork architecture overview (ephemeral sandbox)
sources.extraction_hint: Per-session sandbox created at start and destroyed at end; sessions/files saved to account; local MCP servers do not run remotely
sources._why: The data-persistence boundary that makes Cowork always-latest for delivery but ephemeral for local .local data

sources.name: Manage Claude Cowork plugins for your organization
sources.extraction_hint: Changes take effect on next session or plugin refresh; Sync automatically toggle; auto-sync fires only on a version-bump PR merge, not a direct push
sources._why: The claude.ai/Cowork equivalent of CLI marketplace auto-update; version-bump-PR-merge is a hard release constraint

sources.name: Claude Desktop / Cowork changelog
sources.extraction_hint: Dated fixes for plugin/skill persistence, marketplace auto-refresh, Desktop Extension gating
sources._why: Fast-moving dated signal; several update-relevant behaviors changed month to month in mid-2026

sources.name: Claude third-party connectors with remote MCP
sources.extraction_hint: Add-connector flow, plan gating, OAuth vs request-header auth, org vs individual add paths
sources._why: The best multi-surface true-background channel (a hosted remote MCP server) for claude.ai + Cowork

sources.name: Claude Desktop extensions (MCPB) deployment
sources.extraction_hint: MCPB/.mcpb packaging, directory auto-update vs self-distributed manual reinstall, isDesktopExtensionEnabled gating
sources._why: The Desktop packaged-server update contract; directory bundles auto-update, self-distributed do not

sources.name: ChatGPT developer mode (full read/write MCP client)
sources.extraction_hint: Full read+write MCP client support, plan gating, SSE + streaming HTTP, write actions require confirmation (beta)
sources._why: First-party (fetchable) source for the ChatGPT Tier-B connector path; replaces reliance on the 403-walled help page

sources.name: Connect from ChatGPT (Apps SDK)
sources.extraction_hint: Manual Refresh metadata step, per-conversation write confirmation, all-plans support as of 2025-11-13
sources._why: The live-output-vs-refresh-contract nuance (R1) for ChatGPT MCP connectors/apps

sources.name: MCP and connectors (OpenAI Responses API)
sources.extraction_hint: type:mcp remote tool: server_url, authorization, require_approval; tool list cached per conversation via mcp_list_tools
sources._why: The developer-API Tier-B path that reaches any public remote MCP server with full read/write

sources.name: Model Context Protocol specification 2025-11-25 (stable)
sources.extraction_hint: Streamable HTTP, OAuth 2.1 (RFC 9728/8707/PKCE), tools.listChanged, resources/subscribe, serverInfo.version at initialize; 2026-07-28 is RC only
sources._why: The current stable protocol revision underpinning every Tier-B background-update claim

sources.name: YouTube Studio Analytics export (Advanced Mode CSV)
sources.extraction_hint: Advanced Mode Export current view CSV/Sheets; Table data.csv columns; the only solo-creator revenue + retention source; ~500-row cap
sources._why: The single manual source of YouTube revenue + retention for a solo creator; import_parse parses its CSV

sources.name: Google Takeout: YouTube and YouTube Music export
sources.extraction_hint: video metadata/, playlists/*.csv, subscriptions/subscriptions.csv, history JSON; original video files; NO analytics
sources._why: The creator's own video files + metadata; import_parse reads the Takeout folder layout

sources.name: Instagram Graph API media insights reference
sources.extraction_hint: data[].{name,period,total_value.value}; reels metrics reach/views/saved/shares/ig_reels_avg_watch_time/reels_skip_rate; NO retention, NO hashtag metric
sources._why: Exact per-media insight field names for the live Instagram importer

sources.name: Instagram: review and export your information (DYI)
sources.extraction_hint: JSON export: posts_*.json / reels.json with uri, creation_timestamp, title, media[]; NO insights, NO transcripts; filenames vary by vintage
sources._why: The Instagram export-bundle path; import_parse globs its JSON

sources.name: TikTok Display API v2 Video object
sources.extraction_hint: data.videos[] 15 fields (id, create_time, view_count, like_count, ...); scope video.list; NO retention/hashtag/file-url
sources._why: Exact TikTok Display API video fields for the live importer

sources.name: TikTok: requesting your data (in-app export)
sources.extraction_hint: JSON/TXT export of posted-video list + files; retention curve UI-only, not in export; key casing varies
sources._why: The TikTok export-bundle path; import_parse parses defensively

sources.name: Pinterest API v5 Pin analytics
sources.extraction_hint: keyed by app_type: summary_metrics{IMPRESSION,SAVE,VIDEO_MRC_VIEW,VIDEO_AVG_WATCH_TIME,...}; scopes pins:read; NO retention/transcript
sources._why: Exact Pinterest Pin-analytics metric enums for the live importer

sources.name: faster-whisper (CTranslate2 STT backend)
sources.extraction_hint: CPU/CUDA Whisper via CTranslate2; device=cpu|cuda only (no Apple GPU); needs no system ffmpeg (PyAV)
sources._why: Optional local STT backend for content-import completion; default on Windows/Linux and Intel Mac

sources.name: whisper.cpp (Metal/Core ML STT backend)
sources.extraction_hint: Metal GPU on Apple Silicon; optional Core ML/ANE encoder; brew install whisper-cpp; CLI whisper-cli/whisper-cpp
sources._why: Preferred local STT backend on Apple Silicon (Metal); content-import completion

sources.name: jiwer (WER/MER/WIL accuracy decomposition)
sources.extraction_hint: process_words/process_characters API; RapidFuzz backend changes
sources._why: optional accuracy-validation extra for local STT (P45); wer.py covers the stdlib floor, jiwer adds MER/WIL/WIP decomposition
